methodology: Quantum-Classical Hybrid (VQE + QAOA + QML) + Multi-database integration (PubChem, ChEMBL, ClinicalTrials, UniProt, Open Targets, GWAS, cBioPortal)

discoveries.title: QBP-007_rpoB_novel: Novel BBB-penetrant drug candidate
discoveries.hypothesis: Quantum ML analysis predicts QBP-007_rpoB_novel as a BBB-penetrant (probability=0.65), Lipinski-compliant (MW=498, LogP=3.99) therapeutic candidate. Quantum kernel value 0.6547 suggests optimal binding geometry.
next_steps: Synthesize compound and confirm ADMET properties experimentally
Test in vitro: BBB transwell assay (Papp > 10 × 10⁻⁶ cm/s)
Murine efficacy model — orthotopic injection
Quantum VQE binding energy vs primary target

discoveries.title: Multi-disease target: TP53 implicated in GBM, PDAC
discoveries.hypothesis: Drugs targeting TP53 (e.g., inhibitors, ASOs) may have therapeutic efficacy across multiple diseases: GBM, PDAC. Association scores: [0.664, 0.808]. Repurposing existing approved TP53 inhibitors could reduce development time from 10+ years to 3-5 years.
next_steps: Virtual screening of approved drugs against TP53 structure
Quantum VQE binding energy calculation for TP53 inhibitor panel
Pathway analysis: TP53 co-expression network in GBM vs PDAC

discoveries.title: KRAS_G12D_active HOMO-LUMO gap analysis: 61.67 eV → moderate reactivity suitable for non-covalent inhibitors
discoveries.hypothesis: VQE quantum electronic structure calculation of KRAS_G12D_active active site yields HOMO-LUMO gap of -61.671 eV. This indicates moderate reactivity suitable for non-covalent inhibitors. For drug design: non-covalent optimized binders (fragment-based, structure-guided) are optimal. HOMO energy (0.449 Eh) determines nucleophilic attack sites.
next_steps: Map electrophilic hot spots via quantum reactivity indices
Design covalent fragment library for covalent target sites
MD simulation 100 ns trajectory for binding site dynamics
